title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging
label: mx1_execute_20
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B2-usaco-2023-us-open-gold-tree-merging
Title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging

Problem:
Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree into a final rooted tree.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt: FINAL_ANSWER: The problem is solved by observing that a merge operation on siblings can be viewed as a way to reduce the number of children of a node. To reconstruct the sequence, we use a greedy approach with a stack or a priority queue. For each node, we track its current children. We process nodes in a post-order traversal. For a node $u$, we look at its children. If a child $v$ can be merged w
